jokes.text: That sign says "applesauce." No, I'm kidding. It says "applause."
jokes.setup: Larry points to the applause sign, claims it says something else
jokes.punchline: The absurd misdirection to 'applesauce' before correcting
jokes.explanation: Simple warm-up joke with decent misdirection, but fairly standard talk show opener

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: That sign says "applesauce." No, I'm kidding. It says "applause."

jokes.text: And now, because he couldn't find the exit door... Larry Sanders!
jokes.setup: Standard introduction setup for the host
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating reason for Larry appearing (couldn't find the exit)
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about show business reluctance, fits the show's self-aware tone

dialogue.speaker: Announcer
dialogue.line: And now, because he couldn't find the exit door... Larry Sanders!

jokes.text: Mike Tyson was quoted as saying "I'm in love with myself." Sorry, girls. That's it. That's that joke.
jokes.setup: Mike Tyson quote about self-love
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary that this thin premise is the entire joke
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor acknowledging the weakness of the premise while making that the actual joke

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Mike Tyson was quoted as saying "I'm in love with myself." Sorry, girls. That's it. That's that joke.

jokes.text: They're using the young Elvis stamp for standard mail and the old Elvis stamp for packages over 300 pounds.
jokes.setup: Post office using both Elvis stamps for different purposes
jokes.punchline: Fat Elvis for heavy packages - visual/conceptual match
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor with good visual logic, though could be from any talk show

dialogue.line: They're using the young Elvis stamp for standard mail and the old Elvis stamp for packages over 300 pounds.

jokes.text: Maybe she just didn't tell him where she put his medication.
jokes.setup: Story about elderly couples dying within a week of each other being called 'love'
jokes.punchline: Dark alternative explanation - wife hiding his medication
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy subverting sentimental assumptions with sinister alternative

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Cesario
dialogue.line: Maybe she just didn't tell him where she put his medication.

jokes.text: Arthur, come back soon. We miss your cigar smoke in the hallway.
jokes.setup: Earnest get-well message to absent producer
jokes.punchline: Missing the annoying aspect (cigar smoke) rather than the person
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing the superficial nature of workplace relationships

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Arthur, come back soon. We miss your cigar smoke in the hallway.

jokes.text: Horseshit. Not once in six years did he ever invite me up on the stage. Horseshit.
jokes.setup: Larry just invited temporary producer on stage to be thanked
jokes.punchline: Hank's bitter reaction immediately after the cameras cut
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Hank's jealousy and neediness exposed immediately post-show

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Horseshit. Not once in six years did he ever invite me up on the stage. Horseshit.

jokes.text: ¿Cómo se dice "horseshit"?
jokes.setup: Hank just angrily said 'horseshit' twice
jokes.punchline: Asking how to say it in Spanish, as if he hadn't just said it
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd follow-up showing Hank's scattered mental state

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: ¿Cómo se dice "horseshit"?

jokes.text: my show is being taken over by a guy who looks and sounds like Vanessa Redgrave
jokes.setup: Hank complaining about Jonathan taking over
jokes.punchline: Specific, unexpected comparison to British actress
jokes.explanation: Good specific visual comparison that captures Hank's theatrical way of expressing frustration

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: my show is being taken over by a guy who looks and sounds like Vanessa Redgrave

jokes.text: What's with Phil's fucking nose?
jokes.setup: Larry just had pleasant interaction with Phil
jokes.punchline: Immediate cruel observation the moment Phil leaves
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Larry's fake pleasantness and immediate cruel gossiping

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: What's with Phil's fucking nose?

jokes.text: She isn't. Let's just leave it at that.
jokes.setup: Larry asks about Melanie being on maternity leave, Sam says she isn't pregnant
jokes.punchline: Ominous non-explanation that clearly implies something dark
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor setup that creates uncomfortable curiosity about what really happened

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.line: She isn't. Let's just leave it at that.

jokes.text: Maybe you're too easily rocked.
jokes.setup: Jonathan says Sam 'really rocks' him with enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Larry's dry deflation of the hyperbolic praise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry deadpan that cuts through showbiz enthusiasm with understated sarcasm

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Maybe you're too easily rocked.

jokes.text: Does this mean we have to start wearing jumpsuits because I think only Hank has one?
jokes.setup: Racing metaphors about competing with other talk shows
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation requiring racing uniforms, with Hank detail
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist deflation of corporate speak with specific Hank character detail

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Does this mean we have to start wearing jumpsuits because I think only Hank has one?

jokes.text: If there's one thing I'm known for, it's my complete discretion.
jokes.setup: Sam says the memo shouldn't be distributed
jokes.punchline: Larry claiming discretion while obviously being indiscreet
jokes.explanation: Solid ironic character moment, though the discretion irony is fairly predictable

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: If there's one thing I'm known for, it's my complete discretion.

jokes.text: She tried to kill herself with an overdose of Halcion. She's okay. Let's just leave it at that, okay?
jokes.setup: Larry asking what really happened to Melanie
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of suicide attempt followed by cheerful brush-off
jokes.explanation: Dark payoff to earlier setup, showing Hollywood's casual attitude toward personal tragedy

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.line: She tried to kill herself with an overdose of Halcion. She's okay. Let's just leave it at that, okay?

jokes.text: This business will do that to you.
jokes.setup: Just revealed colleague's suicide attempt
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact explanation as occupational hazard
jokes.explanation: Good dark observation about Hollywood's toll on mental health, delivered with chilling casualness

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.line: This business will do that to you.

jokes.text: It was right on Larry's desk, right in a folder under some papers, in a drawer that was locked. Finding the key was a bitch.
jokes.setup: How he got the memo from Larry's desk
jokes.punchline: Escalating levels of security violation, delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the extreme lengths gone to for office gossip

dialogue.speaker: Writer
dialogue.line: It was right on Larry's desk, right in a folder under some papers, in a drawer that was locked. Finding the key was a bitch.

jokes.text: Unfortunately, Phil's social maladjustment runs so deep that he is unpredictable and volatile. My guess is that he talks badly about Larry behind his back.
jokes.setup: Reading memo critique of Phil
jokes.punchline: Accusation of talking behind Larry's back while literally talking behind Larry's back
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment where the accusation is being proven true in real time

dialogue.line: Unfortunately, Phil's social maladjustment runs so deep that he is unpredictable and volatile. My guess is that he talks badly about Larry behind his back.

jokes.text: Larry is just the kind of asshole to read that and believe it.
jokes.setup: Just read memo saying Phil talks badly about Larry
jokes.punchline: Immediately talks badly about Larry, confirming the memo
jokes.explanation: Excellent immediate confirmation of the memo's accuracy through Phil's own words

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Larry is just the kind of asshole to read that and believe it.

jokes.text: Now I'm sorry I made copies of this for everybody.
jokes.setup: Discussion of how hurtful the memo is
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that she distributed it to everyone
jokes.explanation: Efficient reveal of how workplace drama escalates through gossip

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Now I'm sorry I made copies of this for everybody.

jokes.text: Hank is very sensitive about his age. He's only 34.
jokes.setup: Memo suggests younger sidekick
jokes.punchline: Claiming Hank is 34 (obviously false given Jeffrey Tambor's appearance)
jokes.explanation: Good character joke about vanity and age denial in Hollywood

dialogue.line: Hank is very sensitive about his age. He's only 34.

jokes.text: Hank is not 34.
jokes.setup: Previous claim that Hank is 34
jokes.punchline: Flat contradiction of obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reality check on the absurd age claim

dialogue.line: Hank is not 34.

jokes.text: It's not actually here but it should be.
jokes.setup: Claiming the memo says she's the most competent person
jokes.punchline: Admitting she made that up but asserting it should be true
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing self-advocacy disguised as reading the memo

dialogue.speaker: Darlene
dialogue.line: It's not actually here but it should be.

jokes.text: I went through this same shit once with Sajak.
jokes.setup: Complaining about conflicting plant directives
jokes.punchline: Reference to similar experience with Wheel of Fortune host
jokes.explanation: Good insider reference showing this character's TV history and recurring workplace frustrations

dialogue.speaker: Set designer
dialogue.line: I went through this same shit once with Sajak.

jokes.text: Very funny. Very fuckin' funny.
jokes.setup: Larry asks for someone to put the memo in front of him, no one does
jokes.punchline: Larry's uncomfortable reaction to being ignored/undermined
jokes.explanation: Decent uncomfortable moment showing Larry's lost authority, but fairly standard cringe beat

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Very funny. Very fuckin' funny.

jokes.text: I think you can never have enough vegetation. Ask Sting.
jokes.setup: Defending the plants against memo criticism
jokes.punchline: Random reference to Sting as authority on vegetation
jokes.explanation: Funny non-sequitur that captures Larry's defensive scrambling for any supportive authority

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I think you can never have enough vegetation. Ask Sting.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, we've been canceled.
jokes.setup: Arriving late to tense staff meeting
jokes.punchline: Immediate assumption of worst-case scenario
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank character moment - dramatic catastrophizing from minimal information

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Oh, my God, we've been canceled.

jokes.text: Is Artie dead? Go ahead. Go ahead. I can take it.
jokes.setup: Larry says they're not canceled
jokes.punchline: Hank escalating to death, with dramatic bravery pose
jokes.explanation: Excellent Hank escalation with perfect character-specific dramatic flair

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Is Artie dead? Go ahead. Go ahead. I can take it.

jokes.text: That's an insult. I'm not acting dumb. I don't know what memo.
jokes.setup: Larry tells Hank not to act dumb about the memo
jokes.punchline: Hank's offended denial while proving the point
jokes.explanation: Good character moment where Hank's defense undermines itself

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: That's an insult. I'm not acting dumb. I don't know what memo.

jokes.text: Did Artie die?
jokes.setup: Still confused about the meeting topic
jokes.punchline: Returning to his death obsession despite explanations
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Hank's catastrophic thinking pattern

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Did Artie die?

jokes.text: Do you believe this shit?
jokes.setup: Hank's continued confusion and death fixation
jokes.punchline: Larry's exasperated reaction to camera/audience
jokes.explanation: Basic reaction beat, functional but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Do you believe this shit?

jokes.text: What do I need, a fuckin' security camera? I covered that on page three of the memo.
jokes.setup: Complaining about memo security being breached
jokes.punchline: Revealing he already anticipated this problem in the memo itself
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke showing Larry's paranoid thoroughness came true

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: What do I need, a fuckin' security camera? I covered that on page three of the memo.

jokes.text: Maybe the fucking coffee will become a little bit hotter. From my own experience, fear is a great motivator.
jokes.setup: Larry worried about staff paranoia from leaked memo
jokes.punchline: Jonathan embracing fear as management tool, with petty coffee example
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation of Jonathan's manipulative management philosophy

dialogue.speaker: Jonathan
dialogue.line: Maybe the fucking coffee will become a little bit hotter. From my own experience, fear is a great motivator.

jokes.text: Think I'm attractive and a great dancer, fine. But let's draw the line there.
jokes.setup: Larry saying he wants respect but not fear
jokes.punchline: Adding increasingly specific and narcissistic desires
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Larry's vanity disguised as reasonable boundaries

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Think I'm attractive and a great dancer, fine. But let's draw the line there.

jokes.text: it looks like an old Tarzan movie.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: it looks like an old Tarzan movie.

jokes.text: Horseshit! This isn't about those plants. This is about me. I am those plants, goddamn it.
jokes.setup: Reading criticism of his beloved plants on the set
jokes.punchline: Emotional identification with the plants themselves
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment where Artie's artistic vision becomes deeply personal identity

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Horseshit! This isn't about those plants. This is about me. I am those plants, goddamn it.

jokes.text: Phone the agent. Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death...

jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd juxtaposition of Hollywood panic and religious comfort

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Phone the agent. Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death...

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: He fired me from that QE2 Fourth of July special.

jokes.text: Bullshit. You've gotten an earring to make yourself look younger. What have you done to your nipples?

jokes.explanation: Good escalation of body modification suspicion with uncomfortable intimacy

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Bullshit. You've gotten an earring to make yourself look younger. What have you done to your nipples?

jokes.text: And the left ear is the gay ear. When you wear it low like that, it means you're catching, not pitching.

jokes.punchline: Detailed explanation of supposed gay earring code
jokes.explanation: Period-typical humor that relies on dated stereotypes, shows Larry's inappropriate oversharing

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: And the left ear is the gay ear. When you wear it low like that, it means you're catching, not pitching.

jokes.text: Catching, not pitching.

jokes.punchline: Emphasizing the inappropriate detail
jokes.explanation: Repetitive callback that adds little beyond the discomfort

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Catching, not pitching.

jokes.text: Call me an ambulance.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Call me an ambulance.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: It's Demerol, you ass, and tequila. It's a good mix.

jokes.text: If somebody farts in the Xerox room, I've heard about it.
jokes.setup: Artie claiming nothing gets by him
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific example of his information network
jokes.explanation: Good specific absurd example that captures Artie's omniscient producer persona

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: If somebody farts in the Xerox room, I've heard about it.

jokes.text: I guarantee you there's nothing in there.
jokes.setup: Hank saying he hears something in his head from infection
jokes.punchline: Artie's brutal assessment of Hank's head contents
jokes.explanation: Good cutting insult delivered with perfect deadpan timing

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I guarantee you there's nothing in there.

jokes.text: Drink it, or I'll knock you to the floor, step on your neck and piss in that good ear.
jokes.setup: Hank refusing the drink due to show tonight
jokes.punchline: Artie's elaborately violent threat with ear callback
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating threat that's both absurd and character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Drink it, or I'll knock you to the floor, step on your neck and piss in that good ear.

jokes.text: Never get sick. If there's one cardinal rule in show business, it's 'Never get sick.' It's when you're down, when you show the slightest weakness, that they slip that knife in your back.
jokes.setup: Artie dispensing wisdom while obviously sick himself
jokes.punchline: The irony of giving this advice while violating it
jokes.explanation: Great dark wisdom that's immediately ironic given Artie's current state

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Never get sick. If there's one cardinal rule in show business, it's 'Never get sick.' It's when you're down, when you show the slightest weakness, that they slip that knife in your back.

jokes.text: I have to admire that little Iago... Jonathan Littman.
jokes.setup: Artie analyzing the political situation
jokes.punchline: Sophisticated literary reference calling Jonathan a manipulator
jokes.explanation: Excellent literary reference showing Artie's intelligence and dramatic perspective

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I have to admire that little Iago... Jonathan Littman.

jokes.text: Do you think I skew to an older demographic?
jokes.setup: Hank building up to a deeply personal question

jokes.explanation: Perfect character vulnerability moment, showing Hank's deep insecurity

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Do you think I skew to an older demographic?

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You want to sit there while Kathie Lee prattles on in your good ear about how Frank hates to change Cody's poopy diapers?

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Hello, you worthless piece of shit!

jokes.text: "Cousins Will Fuck" is the first topic.

jokes.explanation: Good absurd parody of trashy daytime TV topics

dialogue.speaker: Artie

jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay connecting literal surgery with metaphorical betrayal

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Yeah, but that knife was wielded by a relative stranger.

jokes.text: swelling it to such a grotesque size that the skull bursts like a ripe honeydew melon. I saw a case like this in Korea once. It was a horrible, painful way to die. Yeah, the screams still haunt me.

jokes.explanation: Good escalating medical horror story that's obviously fabricated but delivered convincingly

jokes.text: You just want people that fear... suck up to you!
jokes.setup: Artie's drunken rant about Larry's motives
jokes.punchline: Combining accusations in garbled way
jokes.explanation: Decent drunken rant that captures Artie's state but isn't particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You just want people that fear... suck up to you!

jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact report of Hank's condition
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy payoff delivered with Larry's typical understated reporting

dialogue.line: Hank just threw up in my wastebasket.

jokes.setup: Jonathan claiming embarrassment about something

dialogue.speaker: Jonathan
dialogue.line: but my enthusiastic and aggressive interest in taking his job and producing this show.

jokes.punchline: Immediate casual admission of deception

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.line: Okay, we're lying.

jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment where Larry's fake politeness cracks completely

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Have I ever told you, Jon, I can't stand the way you talk?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
